reviews.review_title: Fun but waiting on the next chapter
reviews.review_description: This game is fun it runs well. It is a puzzle- hidden object game I wish the next level was ready that is why I only gave it 4 stars
good

Across all types of media and gaming platforms, no genre is as underrepresented as the pirate genre. Which is odd, really. It’s a natural source of entertainment, action, storytelling, and fun. Rarely is it ever boring, and that trend continues here. Hidden Fortune is a swashbuckling good time. It’s not a swordplay epic adventure in the vein of Pirates of the Caribbean, but more of a quest-for-hidden-treasure type of affair with a running narrative. You are some kind of wizard’s apprentice trying to level up with gold and magic while the antagonist, a scruffy billy goat-man, tries to thwart your best efforts. Just writing that sentence sounds bizarre; indeed, the storyline is the weakest aspect of the whole game. It’s confusing and nonsensical but also charmingly goofy, and irrelevant to your enjoyment of the gameplay. There’s two chapters, one free and the other for a few dollars, and both come highly recommended. You set out to complete a series of quests and collect various diary entries, and the story unfolds throughout. Each quest is a set of mini games, many of which share the same basic premise but never get old. Graphically, it’s nothing groundbreaking, although it is an older title developed mainly for the Gear VR, and to be fair, it has a unique art style. It is intentionally cartoonish, and when the characters are on screen, it’s like a pop-up book in VR. It’s a really neat effect, and I found it endearing. The whole thing is pretty immersive despite the lack of realism. It’s a great family game that can be enjoyed by everyone of all ages. I was kind of surprised by how much I got into it, but again, pirate games are often a blast. We need more titles like this, and hopefully someone is taking note.
Good

reviews.review_title: Almost my favorite game on Oculus Go...
reviews.review_description:
This game is great. When I recently purchased my oculus Go this game was already downloaded onto it so I think it is also free. This game is filled with colorful animations, fun mini games, and pretty good in-game hand tracking + visuals. I enjoyed this a lot and it is easily in my top 3 favorite games on the oculus go, I would say it’s probably worth about 10.00$. The reason I took a star away is: I have been having issues lately trying to complete the story, the wizard guy tells me to return to the blimp to track down that bad goat dude or whatever, but it won’t let me continue from there. I have completed both the caves and shanty seas worlds and have found almost all journal entries. Can someone please tell me how, or if, you can continue from here? 2: And also this game has been out for a few years almost and the new world still says coming soon. Do you guys plan on adding that new location still? PLEASE DO, I really want more content and am willing to pay. 
Thanks, lightning7765.
It was very very good
Can be finished in one hour but it's really good

reviews.review_description: One of the best free games on go. So underrated. Only one problem would be lack of instructions, especially the initial controls
Very immersive. I am not into cartoony games but despite that I thoroughly enjoyed this game. The little quests are interesting and capivates your mind.
Good but very difficult in the timer

4.5 - Looks like it is for kids, but there is enough to have some cooldown gaming with few missions (so far) requiring a timer.  The controls are very easy and rewards are quick, with enough light challenge to keep you engaged.  Occasionally an object you want is blocked by another.  Just retry the challenge, as it randomly generates object placement.
